checkAgainst: docs/engine.md §2 Runtime (mode declaration deadline, Away Mode), §4 Accountability ('without renegotiation'), §5 Governance (Retro-only parameter changes); docs/mvp/engine-module.md (tripwire section, default-Green for undeclared days); docs/mvp/error-states.md (whether any human-incapacity path exists beyond technical failures); docs/calibration.md Charter section (any storm-clause provision).
citation: docs/engine.md §2 (mode fixed at Bell, no retroactive amendment; undeclared → Green) and §4 (L2 dead-man fires on absence of completion events; L3 "without renegotiation"); docs/mvp/error-states.md (technical failures only); docs/calibration.md Charter §6 (no storm clause)
description: The dead-man tripwire, L2, and stake execution cannot distinguish avoidance (their target) from involuntary collapse. Mode locks at the bell, undeclared days default to Green (the maximum expectation, harshest for the most depleted user), Away Mode covers only planned travel, retroactive declaration is prohibited, and parameters change only at the weekly Retro — so an injured, medicated, grieving, or mid-crisis user defaults to Green, misses, breaches, and pays. Roughly two-thirds of all near-abandonment events across six lives were spec-correct escalation into circumstance: Ruth's $100 stake executed during grief collapse days after her husband's 45th anniversary ('fined for grieving'); Marcus unconscious on cyclobenzaprine before the bell → breach → $200 stake against a self-employed man losing a week of income ('it fined me for getting hurt'); Devon's L1 into a 39.2° fever on a declared-Red week; Sam's breach caused by one forgotten nightly declaration during a foreseeable conference week he had no way to pre-declare; Ruth's sprained ankle with no path between Retros. Across six lives the stake executed exactly twice — both times against circumstance, never against avoidance. Converged fix shape: Day-0-authored storm clauses/ambush dates written while strong, a witness-confirmable incapacity state that annotates without rescoring and stays the stake, and pre-declarable multi-day non-travel load windows.
title: Escalation and stakes fire on circumstance, not avoidance — no incapacity, storm, or crisis state exists
verifierNote: The gap is real and, per the docs, deliberate: engine.md §2 fixes mode at the Bell and prohibits retroactive amendment as the named anti-escape-hatch feature, §4 fires L2 purely on absence of completion events and executes the stake "without renegotiation" (architecture.md P5: revocable only at governed review; stake review quarterly), while error-states.md and the engine-module error table enumerate only technical failures — a full-corpus grep finds no incapacity, pause, storm-clause, or crisis mechanism anywhere, and Away Mode is defined strictly as pre-specified travel configuration (engine §1, §5). So a user unable to produce any record for two consecutive days (unconscious, hospitalized, grief collapse) breaches and pays by spec, exactly as simulated; MVP's only softener is that L3 is not automated (engine-module.md tripwire step 5), though the design text forbids deliberation at execution. Three peripheral claims are overstated: default-Green is blunted because floor completion equals completion for all adherence math (engine §1; Phase 9 fixture scores an undeclared floor-day 1.0), Retro annotation-without-rescoring already exists (engine §2) though it neither runs mid-week nor stays escalation, and a conscious injured user does have a between-Retro path via daily pre-bell Red declaration — parameters, not load, are Retro-locked. None of this touches the core: nothing in the doc set distinguishes involuntary collapse from avoidance, and the converged fix shape (Day-0 storm clauses, witness-confirmable incapacity state, pre-declarable non-travel load windows) exists nowhere.

checkAgainst: docs/engine.md §4 Accountability (stake clause, witness holds written stake); docs/mvp/engine-module.md (L3/'not automated' language ~line 278, witness.json stake_shared field, 'What the Engine module intentionally is not'); docs/calibration.md Witness arrangement section (stake authoring criteria, executor consent).
citation: docs/mvp/engine-module.md §"The tripwire" step 5 ("L3 is not automated in the MVP. The stake executes by the humans it binds"); docs/engine.md §4 Accountability
description: 'Executed mechanically without renegotiation' has no enforcer: nothing assigns who executes, verifies, records, or re-arms the stake, and the witness has no script when the user renegotiates. Five breach-with-stake events across six lives, zero executions against will: Amara refused Maya's $75 twice (once while holding a puke bucket in Maya's apartment); Elena's 'that doesn't count, we were fighting' stood unchallenged — the Engine's teeth were common knowledge as dead for the remaining 71 days, and unenforceability proved contagious between paired users; Devon paid three days late after floating a mid-quarter suspension his witness had nothing to answer with but memory of the briefing. When the executor loves the debtor, waiving is the loving act and executing makes the witness a debt collector against a suffering person. Compounding gaps: no executor-consent check at Day 0 ('will you actually take this money?'), the witness can be the payment's beneficiary (a conflict of interest that reliably produces waiver), 'written while strong' has no validation (Maya authored hers at 23:40 post-shift), and re-arm within a quarter is untested. The docs internally contradict: engine.md's mechanical execution vs engine-module.md's 'L3 is not automated in the MVP. The stake executes by the humans it binds' (~line 278).
title: L3 has no executor, verifier, waiver record, or re-arm rule — the stake never executes against its actual target
verifierNote: The core execution mechanics are genuinely absent: no doc assigns who executes the stake (engine-module.md tripwire step 5 deliberately stops at "the humans it binds" and "A l2_fired state on /status is the record" — a record of breach, not of execution); no schema has an execution/waiver field (escalation_state is none|l1_fired|l2_fired, with no L3 value); no waiver or refused-execution path exists in engine-module.md's error table or docs/mvp/error-states.md; Day 0 records only that the witness has *seen* the stake (witness.json stake_shared; calibration.md §Witness arrangement "Record that they've seen the stake"), never any beneficiary/executor consent or conflict-of-interest check; and re-arm after an execution is unspecified beyond "the error budget refills" (engine.md §4 restart ritual) plus a quarterly "stake review" (engine.md §5). However, the finding overstates two points: the claimed internal contradiction is weak, because engine.md §4's own stake criteria define "mechanically executable" as "triggered without discussion" (no deliberation), not software-executed, making engine-module.md's non-automation an explicit MVP scoping statement rather than an inconsistency; and renegotiation is normatively covered — architecture.md P5 makes the stake "consent granted in advance... revocable only at governed review," and witness custody/confirmation of the written stake is a closed design decision (engine.md §4 Witness contract, provenance note line 6). What remains open, precisely: executor assignment, execution verification and its record, the witness's script when the user renegotiates anyway, executor/beneficiary consent at Day 0, and stake re-arm semantics within a quarter.

checkAgainst: docs/mvp/engine-module.md (corrections[] ~lines 156-157 and Error states ~lines 297-301, Day record schema, status.json derivation, tripwire section, Commands table — absence of a correction command); docs/mvp/data-model.md (day record, corrections); docs/mvp/error-states.md (harness-down backfill guidance).
citation: docs/mvp/engine-module.md §"Storage additions" (corrections[] lines 156–157), §"Commands", §"The tripwire", §"Error states" rows 297/301; docs/mvp/scope.md S-14
description: Backfilling a completed-but-unrecorded night changes nothing visible: the miss stands in the budget, the streak stays reset, the fired L1/L2 stands, and no correction notice ever reaches the witness. Combined with the fixed 04:00 rollover, completed nights become recorded misses with no contest path: Maya's 04:25 close-out fired an L1 into a day-sleeping night nurse's sleep window; her Day-32 voice-memo floor contributed to an L2 her sister received while physically in the apartment, and the backfill left the fired L2, zeroed streak, and breach intact because no rule says otherwise; Sam's 04:12 close-out briefly showed budget 4/4 for a night that ran — his angriest moment of the quarter, hotter than any true miss; Devon's flu backfill visibly changed nothing, making corrections[] appear decorative. Spurious escalations also spend witness credibility: by Maya's Day-64 L2, Amara modeled the app as 'the thing that's wrong about Maya sometimes.' All six simulations independently flagged backfill-vs-escalation as a [GAP]. Adjacent unwritten math users hit at exactly the moments they check /status: streak formula, whether Red survival extends the streak, breach/budget interplay. No user-facing correction command exists.
title: corrections[]/backfill cannot retract a fired escalation, and the hard 04:00 rollover files completed nights as misses
verifierNote: The core finding is genuine: corrections[] is named four times but has no schema, no fold-into-derived-status rule (unlike observations-module.md §4, which defines refs.corrects and read-time folding), no command that can write one for a past day (/closeout resolves only the current logical day; days/ is adapter-only and chain.json is "the only hand-edited file"), and no interaction rule with escalation_state — the tripwire resets state only via "yesterday completed," and a witness correction notice is foreclosed by the consent amendment's "only autonomous sends" rule. There is also an internal inconsistency: status.json must rebuild byte-for-byte from days/+chain.json yet contains tripwire-set escalation_state. Two peripheral claims are overstated: the 04:00 rollover is instance-overridable (chain.json rollover; engine.md §Defaults), so the night-nurse scenario is configurable away (the one-off overrun with no contest path stands), and breach/budget interplay IS documented (engine.md §SLO, §Restart ritual); Red-day survival counting as completion is derivable from engine.md §Definitions, though the streak formula itself is unwritten. These caveats reduce the periphery, not the gap: P10 and S-14 sanction backfill that the MVP spec cannot mechanically or semantically execute.

checkAgainst: docs/calibration.md (Day-0 required sections, timebox claims, Observations configuration); docs/mvp/engine-module.md Commands section (absence of /enable, correction, export commands); docs/engine.md §4 (Ring 0 'structural' claim); docs/vision.md §7 (rings/consent); docs/mvp/local-runtime.md (who administers the runtime).
citation: docs/mvp/local-runtime.md §"Runtime tree" + §"The privacy boundary of a chat surface"; docs/vision.md §7 "The boundaries that make it safe"
description: Every configuration act — enabling observation kinds, moving bell or tripwire, editing chain.json, issuing corrections, exporting packets — is hand-edited JSON or an undocumented script; the advertised 'ignition tonight, rest in one weekend' ran a full weekend to nine hours for all six personas, and every single life depended on a technical helper for setup and every subsequent change. That helper becomes root: Marcus set up Ruth's always-on box, hand-edited her config.json, and hand-exported six weeks of her capacity-2 and sleep series on day 61 — more access than her contracted witness could ever have, with no contract, briefing, audit trail, or consent record; Elena hand-edited Maya's config twice, guessed an undocumented tripwire parameter, located the packet export script, and fielded both quit drafts, while the official witness contributed a joke and two refused stakes. The witness's Ring 0 is enforced 'structurally, not by preference' — but structural enforcement is meaningless against the person who administers the box. Needs a named, scoped, logged helper role (or an explicit statement that the helper is a conscious Ring 1+ disclosure) plus command surfaces (/enable, correction, export) that make the role less mandatory.
title: Phase 0 is unrunnable without an undocumented technical-helper role that has root access and voids Ring 0
verifierNote: The docs contain no helper/administrator role anywhere (greps for helper/administer/operator/threat-model across docs/ return nothing role-like): local-runtime.md says ~/.lucid/ is simply "Owned by the user," every second-person provision is either witness channel-scoping (engine-module.md §witness.json — "they get no access to ~/.lucid/ at all") or the transit-privacy caveat ("Name this tradeoff to any second user"), and vision.md §7 / architecture.md §5 claim Ring 0 holds "structurally" while enforcement is only projection-layer math plus chat-channel permissions — nothing addresses a host administrator, who bypasses all of it. Meanwhile the design does mandate hand-editing (engine-module.md §Commands: "/chain is deliberately not a command... by hand-editing engine/chain.json"; observations-module.md: config.json "(hand-edited)", disabled kinds hint "add it to observations/config.json"), no export or enable command exists (scope.md §4 declares further commands out of scope; observations.md §7 invokes "an export script" with no specified surface), and engine.md §5 does advertise "five minutes, tonight... one weekend for the rest" — so the helper the finding describes is structurally induced yet completely ungoverned, with the disclosure log (vision.md §7 Record step) covering only user-released aperture exports, not an admin reading the trees directly. Caveats that do not rescue the docs: the finding's "undocumented tripwire parameter" is false (tripwire_time is in the binding chain.json schema, engine-module.md), hand-edited JSON is a documented deliberate choice rather than an omission, and the title's "Phase 0" mislabels what are Phase-2 MVP artifacts (architecture.md §8 Phase 0 is the software-free manual protocol). The core ask — a named, scoped, logged helper role or an explicit statement that the helper is a conscious Ring 1+ disclosure — matches nothing in any doc.

checkAgainst: docs/mvp/error-states.md Safety/Consent section (Sf-2/Sf-3 blocked-draft handling); docs/mvp/agent-contracts.md §3 Reflection (rejected_shape_tags scoping ~lines 310-318, re-proposal rules); docs/mvp/product-principles.md §6 phrase blocklist (external-action verbs, block-never-rewrite).
citation: docs/mvp/acceptance-criteria.md Phase 5 §"Definition of done" + docs/mvp/agent-contracts.md §3 Inputs
description: The most consequential thing the Mirror can notice — a pattern that could read as depression — gets exactly one chance at phrasing (Safety blocks care-referral drafts containing external-action verbs like 'call'/'schedule' and stores them nowhere per Sf-2/Sf-3) and exactly one chance at landing (a rejected shape_tag is never re-proposed). A grieving user's first reflex is rejection; after that the system is permanently mute regardless of fresh evidence. Ruth's Day-48 draft was blocked mid-checkin ('I held that response—'), the Day-51 rephrasing was rejected ('I'm grieving, not depressed') and never returned; her Day-61 PHQ-9 of 9 confirmed the pattern was worth raising — only the non-agent clinician packet carried it forward. Rejection memory is simultaneously too permanent here and too short elsewhere: agent-contracts scopes rejected_shape_tags to the processed-artifact window (~lines 310-318) while product intent reads as permanent non-re-proposal (Elena's rejected shape returned once it aged out of the window) — the two contradict. Needs a re-open rule for professional-care-boundary shapes under accumulating evidence, and a blocklist carve-out or store-for-review path for referral language.
title: The clinical-boundary pattern is structurally one-shot: Safety-blocked drafts are stored nowhere and rejected shapes are silenced forever
verifierNote: The "silenced forever" premise misreads the design: rejection memory is uniformly window-scoped, not permanent. agent-contracts.md §3 builds rejected_shape_tags as "the union of shape_tags from rejected_proposals[] across the window" (last 7 processed artifacts, lines 315-318), and acceptance-criteria.md Phase 5's definition of done says "no rejected shape_tag is re-proposed in the same window" (test 5.4 likewise conditions on window membership) — so Elena's shape returning after aging out is designed behavior, not a contradiction, and Ruth's Day-51 rejection would be eligible again well before Day-61. The three unqualified prose mentions ("future Reflection runs can avoid re-proposing" in steel-thread.md Stage 4, data-model.md §"Insight provenance", agent-contracts.md line 377) are loose wording, not a competing permanent rule. The "one chance at phrasing" claim is also contradicted: Sf-3 (error-states.md) explicitly writes no rejection ("no rejected_proposals[] append") with recovery "User can /checkin again later" — non-storage means a Safety block consumes nothing and the same shape can be re-proposed next session; a low-mood pattern phrased in hypothesis language hits no blocklist rule at all (product-principles.md §6 regex has no "depress" term). Finally, the requested blocklist carve-out/store-for-review for referral language contradicts explicit design: care advice is forbidden agent output (agent-contracts.md §3 Forbidden behavior "consider therapy"; §"Therapist (deferred)": no agent may use therapeutic vocabulary), Safety/Consent is stateless by contract (§4 Forbidden: "Storing state"), and the designed professional-care bridge is the user-invoked clinician packet (architecture.md §6) — the exact path the simulation observed working.

checkAgainst: docs/mvp/engine-module.md 'Consent amendment: pre-committed sends' (the four-template ceiling), tripwire section (l2_fired semantics, re-fire behavior), status.json; docs/engine.md §4 and §6 (restart/return language); docs/mvp/scope.md (whether a fifth pre-consented send class is admissible).
citation: docs/mvp/engine-module.md §"The tripwire (scheduled job)"
description: After L1 and one L2 the system's only remaining output is the nightly bell, which trains notification blindness; 'return after silence is welcomed, not audited' is purely reactive. Sam's Days 38-46: nine days of silence in which the spec's contribution to his return was zero messages — the return was caused by witness Devon breaking the one-ask brief with an off-script second text ('no Lucid, just cousins, dinner?'), sent the same day Devon's own journal read 'system #13?'. A design whose most important retention event requires affectionate rule-breaking has an unwritten load-bearing dependency it cannot audit; Sam's own verdict names the next long silence as the day-365 kill scenario. Whether L2 re-fires during an ongoing lapse and what clears l2_fired are flagged unspecified. Converged fix: one pre-committed, Day-0-consented, self-authored return template firing once on day N of silence, naming only the one-line floor and the no-audit promise — arguably inside the message ceiling's philosophy. Related divergence: amnesty mechanics vs phenomenology — Sam's one-line restart landed perfectly ('Streak 1.', he cried a little), then /status showed the blown budget and foregone gate: 'the receipt for the thing it said it forgave.' No doc addresses what /status should emphasize post-lapse.
title: The ladder ends at L2: the system is mute for the rest of any silence, and return depended on witnesses breaking their brief
verifierNote: Confirmed gaps: (1) L2 re-fire during an ongoing lapse is genuinely unspecified — tripwire step 3 fires on "two consecutive missing/missed days" with no idempotency guard for day 3+, and Phase-10/S-13 fixtures only test the two-day case and recovery; (2) nothing anywhere addresses what /status should emphasize post-lapse — engine.md §4's restart ritual says "nothing is owed," yet status.json is specced to surface budget burn and days-to-gate, and engine.md §6's failure-mode table has no row for multi-day abandonment between L2 and the one-month witness backstop. But the finding overstates twice: what clears l2_fired IS specified (tripwire step 1 resets escalation_state on any completed day), and the docs are not silent post-L2 by accident — engine.md §4 deliberately hands the lapse to humans (witness's one-ask, the mechanically-executed L3 stake, the monthly-heartbeat/one-month-silence backstop). The converged fix is also not "arguably inside the ceiling": scope.md §7, product-principles.md §7 ("Nothing else sends, ever... the three engine templates are the ceiling for autonomous messaging, not a precedent"), and vision.md line 229 ("if you've been away, the welcome happens when *you* open the door... not via a notification") answer the fifth-send question explicitly in the negative — adding it would require a deliberate doc amendment, not a gap-fill.

checkAgainst: docs/mvp/engine-module.md (chain.json bell_time scalar, tripwire scheduled-job config, gate criteria wording); docs/engine.md §2 Runtime and §5 Governance (one-change rule, 'stable timing'); docs/observations.md §3-4 (sleep kind, logical_date anchoring) and §7 Projections (correlate pairing).
citation: docs/mvp/engine-module.md §chain.json + docs/engine.md §5 Governance; docs/observations.md §3 (sleep row) and §7 (Correlates)
description: Four independently fixed clocks misalign catastrophically for non-diurnal schedules, and each misfires separately. Maya (rotating nights): the 21:30 bell fired mid-shift every night week and became noise — she initiated from memory ~40 of 90 nights, quietly destroying the 'user never initiates from memory' premise; the bell-time mode deadline forced 14:00 pre-shift declarations or default-Green nights scored against the maximum; the morning tripwire L1 twice woke her four hours into day-sleep; 'stable timing' as a gate criterion is unmeetable by construction despite ~92% adherence; and /slept cannot represent daytime sleep (logical_date anchoring and night-D→day-D+1 correlate pairing are diurnal-only), so her most clinically relevant series was mispaired for half her weeks until she degraded to quality-only logging. Sam (3am chronotype): the 04:00 rollover was 'the single best chronotype accommodation' until 04:12 converted a completed night into a false miss (see finding 3). The Retro one-change rule technically permits weekly bell moves — a permanent hand-edit-JSON tax nobody pays. Fix shape: a schedule/roster profile that moves bell, declaration deadline, tripwire hour, and rollover together; her verdict names roster-awareness as the single change deciding her year.
title: Clock-fixed mechanics (bell, mode deadline, tripwire, rollover) cannot serve rotating-shift or extreme-chronotype users
verifierNote: The core claim survives verification: all four clocks are independent scalars in chain.json with change capped at one hand-edit per weekly Retro (engine §5, architecture P8), Away Mode — the only schedule mechanism — is travel-only and never moves times (engine §5), "stable timing" in both the ratchet and Gate criteria is wall-clock-defined and thus unmeetable by construction on a rotating roster, and the /slept kind is bindingly diurnal ("the night ending on the morning it is logged"; correlates hardcode night-D→day-D+1). The docs contain no roster/schedule-profile concept anywhere. One caveat: the "extreme-chronotype" prong of the title is overstated — engine §8's instance-overridable defaults let a stable 3am chronotype set all four clocks once at calibration, and Sam's residual failure is the rollover edge already filed as finding 3. The rotating-shift prong (Maya) is a genuine, fully confirmed gap with no partial coverage in any doc.

checkAgainst: docs/mvp/engine-module.md (L2 template/payload, witness.json, channel scoping); docs/engine.md §4 Accountability (what the witness sees and why); docs/vision.md §7 (Ring 0 rationale — privacy framing vs welfare-signal framing).
citation: docs/engine.md §4 (witness contract); docs/calibration.md §Witness arrangement; docs/mvp/engine-module.md §Consent amendment + Phase 10; docs/vision.md §7 (Ring 0)
description: Topline-only L2 (streak + mode) protects content but not meaning or timing. Alarm-without-information: sponsor Big Ed's first question was 'you hurt, or you drinking?' — the rational prior for a contentless alarm is relapse; daughter Naomi produced two calls and three texts within ninety seconds, then demanded weekly journal screenshots. Event-as-content: two consecutive misses correlate with life events, so the send itself broadcasts 'trouble exists, now' — Elena's L2 fired because of a fight about Lucid leaking the marriage, and the escalation delivered exactly the leak Tomas had predicted; the only de-escalating artifact was showing him the payload's poverty of content, which exists by accident. False negative: Ring 0 measures performance, not wellbeing — Elena's worst fortnight (crying in a parked car Day 52, ~20-day unbroken Yellow/Red floor streak) showed her witness nothing but health, while Maya's two L2s fired over nights she had substantially performed; the channel fired false-positive on struggle and false-negative on suffering. Expert witnesses can also invert the trace (Devon read Sam's Yellow-default and Red runs fluently, eroding the dignity-preserving 'I'm fine'). A user-precommitted context line — written while strong, like the stake — could disambiguate without opening Mirror content.
title: Witness-channel signal semantics are wrong twice over: contentless alarms force worst-case inference, while quiet floors hide genuine crisis
verifierNote: The finding is right that the docs offer no in-band disambiguation for an L2 (no pre-committed context line exists) and no guard against witnesses reading quiet floors as wellbeing — the heartbeat's "all quiet — N-day streak" wording (engine-module.md §Consent amendment) actively invites that false-negative, and the one-paragraph briefing (calibration.md §Witness arrangement) never says the signal carries zero welfare information. But the finding understates what is designed: the payload's poverty of content is a binding, test-enforced rule (Phase 10 greps the L2 for zero journal/capacity bytes), not an accident; the Day-0 briefing gives the witness exact signal semantics (two missed chain nights, dead-man) and one job — "ask about it, once" (engine.md §4) — which is the designed disambiguation and directly proscribes the Naomi-style blitz; and Ring 0 is explicitly framed as presence-only, never welfare (vision.md §7). The Devon/Sam mode-trace-reading scenario overstates MVP visibility: the witness gets mode only inside an L2 payload and streak-only heartbeats, with no standing feed (engine-module.md §witness.json). Remaining open: witness-overreaction beyond the one-ask contract, the "all quiet" phrasing's wellbeing conflation, and the absent user-precommitted context line.

checkAgainst: docs/engine.md §3 Telemetry (floor-day ratio 'canary', adherence definition) and §2 (mode definitions, floor-equals-completion); docs/mvp/engine-module.md (status.json ~line 254, gate criteria, /closeout link flags); docs/mvp/acceptance-criteria.md (gate/adherence acceptance tests).
citation: docs/engine.md §3 Telemetry + §5 Governance (Retro rules, Gates); docs/mvp/engine-module.md §status.json and Phase 9 acceptance criteria
description: Pre-declared Yellow floors score 1.0 with no cap on consecutive declarations, no relation to the capacity digit, and no gate consequence, so the headline number rises as practice hollows: Devon ran eleven consecutive knowingly-gamed Yellow days at 91%+ and finished at 93% while calling the 27% floor-day ratio the honest number; Elena's 'prophylactic Yellow' (~14 of 20 days, continued a week after the crisis eased because it was consequence-free) passed the Day-60 gate at 97% — 'I passed the gate I would have designed myself to fail'; Sam cleared Gate 1 at 88% over a ~70% floor-only month including ~4 falsely-flagged retrospective morning links, unverifiable by design. The floor-day-ratio canary has no threshold, no consequence, and no voice anywhere. The documented contradiction — 'floor completion equals completion for all adherence math' vs 'Green = full Chain + Crux' — makes Green strictly dominated ('why would anyone declare Green?') and silently collapses the mode system; Maya's undeclared-Green floor nights scored 1.0 only via fixture behavior. Downstream, no surfaced metric distinguishes integration from a two-minute compliance ritual: Devon's Day-90 numbers read as success while one of three Charter practices was alive. Fix shape: gate displays mandatorily pair adherence with floor-day ratio and raw days; the canary gets a number and a mandated floored Retro question.
title: Mode-relative adherence is already Goodharted: Yellow gaming is free, the floor-day canary has no threshold, and Green-floor scoring is contradictory
verifierNote: The core Goodhart claim is genuine: gates consult only rolling adherence (85%) plus stable timing (engine.md §5 Gates), so a floor-only/prophylactic-Yellow month passes; no doc caps consecutive Yellow declarations, ties them to the capacity digit (captured "for mode fairness" in engine.md §2 but never wired to any check), or gives the floor-day-ratio canary a numeric threshold — "canary trip" in §5 is undefined and its only consequence is "environmental fix"; floor-day ratio is absent from both /status and gate criteria; and the §6 mitigation for "modes becoming the escape hatch" covers only retroactive amendment, not prospective gaming. But two sub-claims overstate: the canary is not voiceless — "rising floor ratio" is explicitly a leading indicator "reviewed at Retro" (engine.md §3) and Tier 3 is a mandatory Retro input (§5); and Green-floor 1.0 scoring is not silent fixture behavior — engine-module.md Phase 9 explicitly documents "an undeclared floor-day scores 1.0 on links... Crux-less Green scoring only applies once the Crux ships post-MVP." What remains open: the engine.md §1 ("floor completion equals completion for all adherence math") vs §2 ("Green = full Chain plus Crux") tension is never reconciled even post-MVP — Crux adherence math is unspecified anywhere, leaving mode declaration score-neutral and Green underdetermined; the canary lacks a number, a gate pairing, and a mandated Retro question exactly as the finding says. Minor: the checkAgainst pointer to acceptance-criteria.md is wrong — it covers Mirror Phases 1–7 only; the gate/adherence tests live in engine-module.md Phases 8–10 and scope.md S-12.

checkAgainst: docs/mvp/agent-contracts.md §3 Reflection (resonance gate, what is recorded on non-answer, session definition); docs/observations.md §6 Curiosity (the existing backoff ladder to mirror); docs/mvp/scope.md (proposal cadence, 'session' scoping).
citation: docs/mvp/steel-thread.md §Stage 4 — Validation (hard rules); contrast docs/observations.md §6 Curiosity
description: Unanswered proposals write nothing, so the user's strongest fatigue signal — stopping answering — leaves zero trace and the stream continues at constant cadence regardless of hit quality. Across six lives: 49 proposals, roughly half true-but-useless restatements, ~1.4 per user-quarter producing behavior change; three of six users parodied 'Does this resonate?' by week 9. Users abandon /checkin to avoid the gate, suppressing exactly the capture that would improve proposals: Marcus's /checkin went from ~2/week to zero for ten days; Elena drafted a quit-the-Mirror message on Day 40 and the record shows only two fewer artifacts; Devon's ignore streak (~3 asks/week at 23:40 during 70-hour weeks) left nothing the system could learn from. No sensitivity rule governs timing after emotionally loaded entries: Sam's accurate Maya proposal 16 days post-breakup cost ten days of /checkin avoidance (while proving the Engine/Mirror decoupling — his Engine was unaffected). The asymmetry is glaring: curiosity questions already have exactly the needed ladder (7-day suppression after ignore, retire after 3 ignores); Reflection proposals have none. 'Session' is itself undefined, so per-session caps mean nothing.
title: Resonance-gate fatigue is invisible by design: silence records nothing, and proposals have no cadence governor or timing sensitivity
verifierNote: The core gap is real and explicit: steel-thread §Stage 4, mvp/architecture.md §Per-session sequence step 8, agent-contracts.md §"How contracts compose", and data-model.md §Insights all specify that an unanswered proposal records nothing anywhere (not even the ephemeral scheduler state the curiosity ladder uses), and no backoff, fatigue, or post-emotional-entry timing rule exists for Reflection proposals — the asymmetry with observations.md §6's 7-day/3-ignore ladder is genuine, and adaptive timing lives only in technical-spec.md §Adaptive Evolution, which README §"What this MVP is not" excludes. But the finding overstates in two places: "session is undefined" is contradicted by data-model.md §"Sessions and channel memory" and local-runtime.md's Discord mapping (session ≈ thread, one record per command invocation), so the one-proposal-per-session cap is meaningful; and "constant cadence" mischaracterizes a strictly pull-based design — proposals occur only inside user-invoked /checkin, never as pings, with rejected-shape suppression (agent-contracts §3), first-class no_pattern outcomes, and /log as a Reflection-free capture path. What remains open: no ignore/backoff ladder for unanswered proposals, no proposal-frequency knob (curiosity has a user-set budget where 0 is valid, per calibration.md; proposals have nothing), no timing-sensitivity rule after emotionally loaded entries, and the fact that the only fatigue escape preserving structured capture does not exist (/log skips Structuring, so avoiding the gate also removes material from the pattern window).

checkAgainst: docs/engine.md §4 Accountability (the 'ask about it, once' clause and its scope); docs/mvp/engine-module.md (witness briefing/consent flow, witness.json); docs/calibration.md Witness arrangement (briefing script, any conduct guidance beyond the one ask).
citation: docs/engine.md §4 Witness contract; docs/calibration.md §Witness arrangement; docs/mvp/scope.md S-13; docs/mvp/engine-module.md §witness.json
description: Ask-once assumes an arm's-length witness. When the witness already knows everything, the ask is surveillance theater: Amara delivered 'your app says you missed two days?' while holding a puke bucket in Maya's apartment, nursing the child who caused the misses — less information than she had, delivered with institutional coldness. When affection overwhelms restraint, the contract silently collapses: Naomi's ask-once lasted ninety seconds (two calls, three texts) and nothing noticed, recorded, or repaired the breach of briefing — identically-briefed Big Ed asked exactly once; the variable was the person, and no selection guidance exists. When the witness honors the brief, it becomes a cage: Maya sat a week on whether fifteen years of friendship licensed a second question. And the retention-critical acts all exceeded the brief: Devon's Day-45 second text, the dinner, the 'now you're clean' stake framing — a witness who followed the contract exactly would have watched Sam disappear. The docs never say whether ask-once is per-escalation or per-silence-episode, whether a second contact is violation or tolerated overage, whether a witness with independent knowledge may skip the ask, or what the witness may do with unresolved worry.
title: The ask-once witness contract breaks in both directions under real intimacy, and every rescue exceeded the brief
verifierNote: One of the finding's four enumerated ambiguities is resolvable from the docs: the ask is bound to the trigger event — engine.md §4 says "when L2 fires, ask about it, once" and scope.md S-13 pins "2 consecutive → exactly one L2," so per-escalation and per-breach-episode coincide by construction, and the month-of-silence backstop is likewise event-scoped. The finding also overreads the witness's role: retention is carried by the stake (L3, executed mechanically) and the restart ritual, not by witness persuasion, so "a contract-faithful witness would have watched Sam disappear" misattributes the design's rescue mechanism. But the rest is genuinely silent: calibration.md's Witness arrangement is only name/channel/briefing-date/confirmation plus a one-paragraph brief — no selection guidance (distance, temperament, intimacy), no statement of whether "exactly one job... once" is a ceiling (second contact = violation) or a floor (minimum check), nothing for a witness with independent knowledge or co-present in the miss, and nothing about what the witness may do with unresolved worry after the ask. The system records the witness's confirmation verbatim (engine-module.md witness.json) but specifies no notice, record, or repair for briefing breaches in either direction, and never acknowledges that the brief is unenforceable human conduct rather than system behavior.

checkAgainst: docs/mvp/engine-module.md 'Consent amendment: pre-committed sends' (heartbeat template, Phase-0 manual delivery); docs/engine.md §4 (month-of-silence backstop wording); docs/engine.md §6 Failure modes (whether heartbeat-at-streak-0 / post-L2 is acknowledged).
citation: docs/engine.md §4 (Witness contract); docs/mvp/engine-module.md §"Consent amendment" and §"The tripwire" step 4
description: 'All quiet — N-day streak' cannot be sent truthfully in a month containing an L2 or an executed stake, and it failed as written in 100% of observed sends. Users skip it (Devon: due one day after his executed stake, streak 3; sent nothing; nothing noticed — no doc says what a missed heartbeat means), rewrite it (Ruth both months; Marcus refused the false template days after his L2 and freehanded an honest line), or send technically-true fiction (Sam's 'all quiet — 15-day streak' twenty-two days after an L2 the recipient personally received). Every edit silently dissolves the fixed-template discipline the send class depends on and erodes the dead-man value of the channel. The month-of-total-silence backstop fails two more ways: non-user witnesses forget it exists (Amara answered a heartbeat with '??'; only fellow-user Maya ever operated the backstop correctly), and informal side-channels make silence never register — the cousins' screenshot trading left Devon feeling current while the specced channel was dead, so the real L2 fired into his funding crunch with no warning glide, and the docs never say whether out-of-band contact resets the silence clock. Meanwhile the quarter's most-requested feature (Ruth's day-90 top ask) was exactly a gentler, weekly, content-free heartbeat. Needs: a non-quiet variant, cadence options, paraphrase rules, witness re-briefing, and side-channel semantics.
title: The heartbeat is false-by-design after a loud month, Phase-0 manual send dissolves it, and side-channels silently defeat the month-of-silence backstop
verifierNote: The core defect is real: the heartbeat template is fixed as "all quiet — N-day streak" (engine-module.md §"Consent amendment") and tripwire step 4 posts it unconditionally on the first run of each month — steps 3 and 4 are not exclusive, so on a month-first morning during an active breach the witness channel receives an L2 and an "all quiet — 0-day streak" together; neither engine.md §6 failure modes nor engine-module.md's error-states table acknowledges heartbeat-at-streak-0/post-L2, and there are no paraphrase rules, witness re-briefing (briefing is explicitly "once, at Day 0"), or explicit side-channel statement. But three sub-claims misread the docs: (1) a missed heartbeat's meaning IS specified — engine.md §4 defines the backstop as "no escalation and no heartbeat for a full month," so Devon's skipped heartbeat one day after an executed stake (an L2 month) correctly triggers nothing, and the silence clock is defined strictly over specced sends, meaning out-of-band contact does not, by the letter, reset it; (2) the manual send is Phase-0-only by explicit doc statement, and the MVP automates the heartbeat in the tripwire job, structurally closing the dissolution path; (3) Sam's post-restart "all quiet — 15-day streak" is consistent with the template's present-state, streak-only payload sent to a witness who already received the L2 — the doc never claims the heartbeat summarizes the month. Remaining open: heartbeat content semantics under l2_fired/streak-0 (including the same-morning L2+all-quiet collision), Phase-0 template-discipline enforcement, witness re-briefing cadence, an explicit out-of-band-contact rule, and any cadence/variant options (monthly is fixed and not among engine.md §8's overridable defaults).

checkAgainst: docs/mvp/agent-contracts.md §3 Reflection (7-artifact window, artifact eligibility, session boundaries); docs/calibration.md Intake corpus / Calibration sections (voice-memo capture, Phase-0 backfill); docs/observations.md §7 Projections (coverage-floor discipline lacking a Reflection analogue); docs/mvp/scope.md (consolidation deferral).
citation: docs/mvp/agent-contracts.md §3 Reflection (inputs: count-based 7-artifact recent_window, no coverage rule) vs docs/observations.md §7 Correlates (coverage floor); docs/mvp/scope.md §3 (close-out → Structuring → Reflection flow)
description: The target population produces the least material: Maya at capacity 2 wrote ~85% single-line entries and ran /checkin 5 times in 90 days — five proposals, two wrong or artifactual, one meaningful insight per month; no doc says whether the close-out line is even a proposal-eligible session, and no low-effort ramp exists between the one-line floor and a full /checkin. The richest material never enters: Ruth's garden voice memos and hospital-week recording stayed outside the Ledger seven weeks — calibration.md names voice memos as Phase-0 capture but specifies no ingestion or backfill path. What does enter is systematically skewed: a 21:30 close-out samples end-of-day depletion, so Elena's Ledger became a grievance archive (~70% of entries about Tomas in complaint register) with no dominance threshold, register detection, or designed moment asking whether the record is replacing the relationship — she nearly quit from shame on Day 66, and the saving Day-52 proposal fired only because crisis compressed the pattern into one 7-artifact window. Devon's work-monoculture windows degraded proposals to summaries. Missingness is unmodeled — absence of logging reads as absence of the thing: Maya's Day-87 'less pain on workdays' proposed the ER cures her shoulder (she cannot log mid-shift); Ruth's Day-85 lag correlation ran causality backwards; correlates have a coverage floor (observations.md §7) but prose patterns have no analogue.
title: Nightly close-out capture skews and starves the Mirror: thin, depleted, monoculture corpora produce banal or false patterns with no missingness model
verifierNote: The core epistemic gap is confirmed: observations.md §7 gives correlates a coverage floor precisely because "logging density is itself correlated with symptom state," but Reflection.propose (agent-contracts.md §3) has no analogue — its window is the last 7 processed artifacts by count regardless of time span (validating the crisis-compression observation), there is no unlogged-day model, no dominance/register/skew mechanism anywhere in the doc set, and Reflection cannot even condition on depletion because the capacity digit is sanctuary-locked to the engine tree (engine-module.md §Day record: "capacity and limiter_tag live only here"); the only mitigations are hypothesis framing, the resonance gate, and the honest no_pattern outcome. However, three sub-claims misstate the docs: (1) the close-out line's proposal eligibility is not unspecified — scope.md §3's flow diagram routes the journal line raw → Structuring → Reflection proposes ≤1 pattern, and §9 says journal lines "give Structuring a real corpus on day one" (though this genuinely conflicts with agent-contracts.md §"How contracts compose," where the journal line enters raw/ "as an ordinary entry" and /log-style entries skip Reflection at capture time — an internal inconsistency, not silence); (2) voice-memo ingestion is a named, deliberately deferred seam (mvp/architecture.md §Extension points: /voice + transcription skill; engine-module.md /closeout step 5), and backfill of transcribed material is specified via /bootstrap's bitemporal occurred_at (mvp/architecture.md command table; technical-spec.md §Bootstrapping "re-enter anytime"), so only audio ingestion is missing, acknowledged rather than silent; (3) /log is an anytime, zero-question capture sitting between the one-line floor and full /checkin, weakening the "no ramp" claim, though no elicitation mechanism exists for thin prose (curiosity in observations.md §6 covers observation kinds only). Remaining open: a missingness/coverage discipline for prose proposals, any corpus-skew or dominance handling, resolution of the scope.md-vs-agent-contracts.md inconsistency on when /log//closeout entries trigger Reflection, and the MVP audio-ingestion hole.

checkAgainst: docs/engine.md §4 Accountability (witness role, confirmation requirement, briefing); docs/mvp/engine-module.md (witness.json, confirmation flow, what blocks when unconfirmed); docs/calibration.md Witness arrangement (selection guidance, briefing script); docs/mvp/error-states.md (witness non-response coverage).
citation: docs/mvp/engine-module.md §"Acceptance criteria" Phase 10 & §"Error states"; docs/engine.md §4 "Witness contract" / §5 "Day 0 — definition of done"; docs/calibration.md §"Witness arrangement"
description: A witness who never confirms silently disables the entire escalation ladder with no warning surface: Devon's cousin Sam-adjacent case — a witness who never sent confirmation — killed that system in under seven weeks (L2 structurally blocked, escalation toothless, quiet evaporation; the user never knew why), the only full quit observed across all simulations. No revocation, replacement, re-briefing, second-ask, or second-witness procedure exists anywhere. There is no capacity check on the receiving human: Sam's L2 landed on Devon five days after Devon's own executed stake, two days before his bridge round closed — for Days 41-45 the entire accountability system for a spiraling person was one exhausted founder deciding privately whether to exceed his brief. Selection guidance is absent for configurations that predictably fail: stressor-entangled witnesses (Ana mid-term-sheet, coupling the channel to the crisis causing the misses); recruiter-witness stacking evangelist/creditor/judge on one power gradient (the $50 stake Venmo'd to the mentor personally, mid-grief, while the mentor read the mentee's silence as a referendum on his own judgment); family-vs-peer tradeoffs demonstrated cleanly by identically-briefed Big Ed vs Naomi. Briefings have no variants for witnesses whose pre-existing intimacy sits above or below Ring 0 (the sponsor found it insulting; the daughter found it terrifying), and physical-proximity capability is never considered — every welfare check that mattered for two older adults living alone happened outside the system.
title: Witness lifecycle and selection are load-bearing and unspecified: a silent witness disarms the whole ladder, and conflicted configurations go unchecked
verifierNote: The headline mechanism is contradicted by the docs: a never-confirming witness cannot "silently" disarm the ladder, because Day 0 gates program start on witness confirmation (engine.md §5 "Day 0 — definition of done"), an L2 attempt against unconfirmed witness.json is "blocked + user notified" (engine-module.md Phase 10; scope.md S-13), an unreachable witness channel falls back to an explicit user message ("L2 fired but couldn't reach <witness> — you owe the message", engine-module.md error table), L1 keeps firing regardless, and the monthly heartbeat exists precisely so total silence is itself a briefed signal (engine.md §4). "The user never knew why" and "quiet evaporation" misread the design. However, everything past confirmation is genuinely unspecified: a witness who confirmed and then disengages is undetectable (no L2 receipt/response tracking, no second-ask, no witness-liveness check — the backstop depends entirely on the witness acting); there is no revocation, replacement, re-briefing, or second-witness procedure anywhere (quarterly governance reviews the stake but not the witness, engine.md §5); there is no capacity or availability consideration for the receiving human; and calibration.md §"Witness arrangement" is four record fields plus one fixed briefing paragraph — zero selection guidance on stressor entanglement, power gradients, family-vs-peer, intimacy mismatch with Ring 0, or physical proximity. Those remaining gaps are real and load-bearing for a Day-0-required role; the "stake Venmo'd to the mentor" configuration is not doc-prescribed (the witness holds the written stake, not the money, engine.md §4) but nothing in the docs would prevent it.

checkAgainst: docs/mvp/agent-contracts.md §3 Reflection (insight frontmatter fields, surface_for_recall contract, contradiction handling); docs/mvp/acceptance-criteria.md (S-15 definition); docs/mvp/scope.md (cron optionality, no-autonomous-Mirror-outreach tension).
citation: docs/mvp/agent-contracts.md §3 Reflection + §"How contracts compose"
description: No mechanism routes an accepted insight anywhere: Sam's gold Day-55 insight (shrinking entries precede disappearing — a warning-shaped early-warning) had to become a human pact with his witness, entirely outside the system. /reflect, the only surface that re-presents validated insights, is manual with no push (the cron hook is optional) and ran zero times during Elena's Days 29-56 — exactly the dip-plus-hospital arc where the Day-25 and Day-41 insights mattered most; she rebuilt cadence only with her own phone alarm, and nothing noticed the four-week silence. Politeness-accepts are indistinguishable from life-changing ones in the record (Devon accepted the gym correlate 'out of politeness'; three of Ruth's five accepts were shrugs, identical on disk to the Day-22 insight that changed her dinners for 68 days), making framework rotation and any measurement of Mirror value impossible — S-15 counts validated insights only. Soft contradictions, among the best observed moments (Sam's Day-18 produced his most honest writing of the month), store nothing and evaporate. Fix shape: an acted-on/behavior-link field distinct from last_confirmed_at, updated at /reflect; a re-surfacing cadence that survives load; a defined consumer for warning-shaped insights.
title: Accepted insights are write-only: nothing consumes them, /reflect dies under load, and acted-on value is unmeasurable
verifierNote: The headline "write-only, nothing consumes them" is overstated: accepted insights have two defined consumers — /reflect (reflection.surface_for_recall) and /ask (reflection.answer_grounded over the cap-50 accepted slice), plus the stale-window fallback in error-states.md R-7 — and the manual-/reflect/no-push design is an explicit, reasoned decision (local-runtime.md §"Local cron / consolidation": cron optional, "never DMs the user"; product-principles.md §7 makes the three Engine templates a hard ceiling on autonomous messaging, so the finding's "re-surfacing cadence that survives load" via push would contradict a core principle, not fill a silence). What remains genuinely open: (1) soft contradictions truly persist nothing — the /checkin compose sequence writes only on accepted/nuanced/rejected, and steel-thread.md §"Soft contradiction" says an un-engaged gap "is not re-surfaced unless fresh evidence reopens it," with no record kind or capture path defined even when the user engages; (2) the insight schema (data-model.md §"Validated insights") has confirm/soften/retire and verbatim user_response_text but no acted-on/behavior-link field, so acceptance quality is only distinguishable via later /reflect status_history — a mechanism that itself dies when /reflect lapses, and nothing in the docs notices a lapse; (3) scope.md §8 S-15 measures only the existence of ≥1 validated insight, and no metric measures acted-on Mirror value. The MVP also has no consumer for warning-shaped insights: the full spec names exactly these seams (engine.md §3 Tier 3 "shrinking entries" canary reviewed at Retro; engine.md §"Crux protocol" Mirror-offers-candidate-via-resonance-gate), but scope.md §7 defers Retro/Crux tooling to manual practice, leaving the bridge human-only in the MVP. (The "framework rotation" complaint is moot — frameworks are an explicitly deferred non-goal per agent-contracts.md §Framework and scope.md §7.)

checkAgainst: docs/mvp/engine-module.md 'Consent amendment: pre-committed sends' (template definitions and variants), '/closeout sequence' (skip semantics), Day record schema (memo/annotation field absence); docs/engine.md §5 Governance (Retro 'may annotate a miss but never rescores it'); docs/mvp/product-principles.md §6 (voice standards applied—or not—to Engine copy).
citation: docs/mvp/engine-module.md §"Consent amendment: pre-committed sends" and §"The tripwire (scheduled job)"; docs/engine.md §2; docs/mvp/product-principles.md §6 "Phrase blocklist"
description: The pre-committed template set lacks even deterministic branches for states the Engine already knows. L1 'tonight is a must' fired into day four of Devon's 39.2° flu on a declared-Red week — he came close to disabling the tripwire cron, the system's silent death — and read as cruelty the morning after Ruth's anniversary crash (habituation, not wording, eventually rescued it). /closeout skip, the honest zero the data model deliberately distinguishes from silence, produces byte-identical consequences: Sam's deliberate 'can't tonight' at 11pm post-breakup bought the same L1, streak zero, and budget spend as hiding — 'why did I bother telling it'; he never used skip again, corroding the one behavior the system most needs from a shame-prone user. Marcus's L1 after choosing his son over the close-out was tonally absurd, and the ledger permanently encodes that proud miss identically to his drugged-and-despairing Day 58 — engine.md permits Retro annotation of a miss but defines no mechanism, field, or command, and the day record has no memo field. None of these need an LLM: one more deterministic variant each (Red-declared L1, skip-acknowledging L1) preserves the no-autonomous-variation principle. The verbatim strings are load-bearing and live only in the repo, escaping doc review entirely.
title: Fixed templates are context-blind at retention-deciding moments, and honest confession costs exactly what hiding costs
verifierNote: The core gaps are real: the L1 is a single fixed template ("last night was a miss; never miss twice; tonight is a must; the floor, named") with no deterministic branch on declared mode or on `missed: true` — the tripwire treats "No record, or missed: true" identically (engine-module.md §"The tripwire" step 2), and only the L2 template carries declared mode, despite engine.md §2's own legitimacy principle that "a push calibrated to declared capacity has standing"; likewise engine.md §2 permits the Retro to "annotate context on a miss" and engine-module.md's error-states table names "Retro annotation" as a recovery path, yet no command, flow, or day-record field implements it (the schema has only `corrections[]`, defined for backfill/corrections, never wired to annotation). However, three sub-claims are overstated: skip's identical escalation is explicit, deliberate design, not oversight ("does not suppress escalation" — scope.md §4 command table; "self-report is never the trigger" — engine.md §4 dead-man semantics); the templates do not "escape doc review entirely" — their content fields are doc-specified and product-principles.md §6 requires Engine template output to be "pre-vetted against this list at commit time" (though that regex vet checks diagnostic/performative language, not situational tone); and on a declared-Red day the L1's named floor is already the one-sentence survival link (engine-module.md chain.json note), softening but not answering the tonal charge. What remains open: no mode-aware or skip-acknowledging deterministic L1 variants, no defined annotation mechanism/memo field despite two doc references to it, and no doc home for the verbatim template strings themselves.

checkAgainst: docs/observations.md §7 Projections (packet contents, render→review→release aperture, export invocation, first-window, gap-list items ~15-16); docs/mvp/observations-module.md (command surface); docs/mvp/scope.md (export scope).
citation: docs/mvp/observations-module.md §"Commands (router additions)"; docs/observations.md §7 "Clinician packet" and §10 Defaults
description: The packet changed a medical trajectory — Maya's derived med-frequency header ('you're taking 400mg most days?', 41 doses/70 days) plus pain/interference series converted a likely wait-and-see ortho visit into same-day injection, an MRI order, and a named partial supraspinatus tear one-to-two visit cycles early — and a mental-health trajectory: Ruth's packet (capacity/sleep series, notes excluded by default) turned a deflectable 'how are you managing' into a PHQ-9 of 9 and a grief-group referral producing her quarter's largest gains ('the graph said what I wouldn't'). It is the strongest observed expression of Lucid's value proposition: making the user legible to their existing human infrastructure — and it is also the sanctioned answer to the depression-adjacent boundary the Mirror can only raise once (finding 5). Yet no command invokes it, the first-ever export window is undefined ('since previous export' when none exists), no surface ever mentions it exists, and both real exports shipped only because a friend knew where the undocumented script lived. It also omits user-elected clinical context: Marcus's 'three years sober, don't offer me Percocet' — the single most decision-relevant datum at an opioid prescribing decision — traveled by voice only; default exclusions could present a recovery patient as having no relevant history, and taken:false regimen handling is an open gap in the doc's own list.
title: The clinician packet is the study's highest-value artifact and has no command, no first-window rule, no discovery path, and no clinical-disclosure field
verifierNote: The four central absences are real. (1) No command: the MVP router table (observations-module.md §Commands) defines only /pain, /ate, /drank, /bm, /mood, /slept, /obs, /obs where, /day; the packet's invocation exists only as an unnamed "export script" parenthetical in observations.md §7's cross-tree rule, with no name, syntax, or surface anywhere (phase 12 criteria and scope.md S-18 test the rendered output, never how it is invoked). (2) First window: §7 says "default: since the previous packet export" and §10 says "window = since last export" — no doc defines the window when no prior export exists. (3) Discovery: no MVP surface mentions the packet; vision.md §7's "want the packet drafted?" reminder belongs to the post-MVP aperture system (vision.md §"What Lucid needs" and scope.md §7 non-goals defer it). (4) Clinical-disclosure: the header derives only from injuries registry, med events, and episodes; standing user-elected context like recovery status has no schema home (injuries' "clinical notes" don't cover it), mitigated only weakly by "user-selected subset of everything else" and the vision-level review-edit step. One sub-claim is wrong as stated: there is no "gap list" in observations.md (checkAgainst's "items ~15-16" does not exist), so taken:false regimen handling is an unacknowledged ambiguity in §7's "most recent dose per distinct med," not a doc-admitted open gap.

checkAgainst: docs/engine.md §1-2 (chain/link model, one-chain-until-Gate-1, opportunity habits 'never quota'd'); docs/mvp/scope.md §7 and docs/mvp/engine-module.md 'What the Engine module intentionally is not' (multi-chain refusal and rationale); docs/mvp/engine-module.md '/closeout sequence' (retrospective link flags).
citation: docs/engine.md §1-2, §5; docs/architecture.md §4; docs/calibration.md §"Portfolio snapshot", §"Active configuration"
description: A user whose Charter names three committed practices can put exactly one under Engine accountability — and practices time-of-day-incompatible with the single evening chain or non-daily in cadence structurally cannot receive teeth, in the MVP effectively forever. Devon: deep work died silently in week 4 (recovered only via a 'target card' Link bolted to the bedtime chain), strength training ended the quarter at baseline, and his top Day-90 request was a morning chain the MVP has no answer for; he hacked /obs into a pseudo-tracker by Day 3 — users route around the boundary. Sam: the morning meds+thesis anchor — the actual point of his system — was faked as a retrospective 1am checkbox, falsely flagged ~4 nights because at 1am the morning is an abstraction; the meds half was saved by physical staging (pill bottle on the keyboard), not the link; his most-wanted capability was a second tiny morning bell. Marcus: the weekly program call, core recovery hygiene, fits neither daily chains nor never-quota'd opportunity habits — skipped 3 of 5 weeks during the dip with zero system visibility, rescued by a Mirror insight rather than any Engine mechanism. The docs never address non-daily cadence, multi-time-of-day binding, retrospective link scoring, or how a user's other commitments are supposed to live.
title: One chain until Gate 1 leaves most of the Charter toothless: morning, weekly, and non-daily commitments have no home
verifierNote: The finding understates what the docs already provide: single-chain scarcity is a deliberate, rationale-carrying decision ("Chains are deliberately scarce... additions are earned" engine.md §2; calibration.md "One chain only until Gate 1"; scope.md §7; engine-module.md §"intentionally is not"), and other commitments DO have documented homes — portfolio statuses with quarterly Hearings (architecture.md §4, calibration.md §Portfolio snapshot: "make 'not chosen now' stop compiling as 'failed again'"), Config for Sam's meds (the staged pill bottle is the documented mechanism itself, engine.md §1: "a staged object... a fixed supplement anchor"), opportunity-habit tallies, and the Crux protocol, a mandatory-contact slot placed in the peak-energy window (morning-capable) that fits Devon's deep work, kept as a manual practice this phase (engine.md §2; engine-module.md). The /obs route-around is also anticipated and deliberately blocked (observations are inventory, never obligation; architecture.md §4b forbids teeth outside the Gate process), and "the Charter names three practices" misreads the Charter, which holds identity/non-negotiables/stake, not a practice list (calibration.md §The Charter). What remains genuinely open: (1) non-daily cadence — every teeth mechanism (SLO, chain, gates, tripwire) is daily, so Marcus's weekly call has no accountability home anywhere in the docs; (2) second-chain/multi-time-of-day mechanics — "one Chain until the first Gate" implies later chains, but gates grant only "one new Link or activate one Parked item" (engine.md §5), Links cue sequentially from a single Bell, and neither the full spec nor the MVP schema (one bell_time, one tripwire) ever specifies how a morning chain would exist; (3) scoring a link executed hours before close-out is unaddressed, though it is out-of-model usage. "Effectively forever" is overstated for an explicitly narrow 30-day proof (scope.md §10: "It will feel narrow. That is the point"), but sub-gaps (1) and (2) are real doc silences.

checkAgainst: docs/engine.md §5 Governance (Retro spec, 20-minute cost, avoidance sort, Crux protocol, simplify-only rule); docs/mvp/engine-module.md Day record schema (Crux field absence); docs/mvp/error-states.md (missed-Retro / missed-governance handling).
citation: docs/engine.md §5 Governance + §6 failure-mode table; docs/mvp/engine-module.md §"Day record" and §"Consent amendment"
description: The weekly Retro is the only place parameters change and the only mechanism that can simplify the system when adherence drops — and it costs a fixed 20 minutes, has no floor version, no failure path when skipped, and per the message ceiling nothing may even mention its absence. Devon skipped weeks 5-6 precisely when adherence <5/7 should have fired the simplify-only rule; his Day-45 near-quit happened in that governance vacuum, and both observed hollowing near-deaths (Devon D45, Elena D40) coincided with skipped Retros. The subsystem designed to save the system under load is the first casualty of load — in a product otherwise obsessive about floors. Compounding: Crux has no field in the day record, no adherence consequence, and no surface beyond the user's memory. Devon's Crux was effectively abandoned by week 4 with zero record it ever existed while every surfaced metric read success; Elena's highest-charge item ('schedule the couples-counseling consult', self-nominated Day 24) sat in the avoided-task queue untouched through the Day-90 postmortem, because the Retro avoidance-sort that would have forced the Crux-or-retire decision only works if the Retro happens. Converged fix: a floored 5-minute Retro, triggered mandatorily when the floor-day-ratio canary trips (finding 9), plus a Crux/memo field in the day record.
title: Governance has no floor and no failure path: Retros die exactly under load, and Crux is structurally invisible
verifierNote: The core claims are genuine gaps in the canonical design, not just MVP scoping: engine.md §5 fixes the Retro at 20 minutes with no floored variant (in a spec otherwise saturated with floors), §1 makes the Retro "the only place runtime parameters change," §5 makes the simplify-only rule and §3 makes canary review Retro-outputs, and neither engine.md §6, engine-module.md's error table, nor error-states.md defines any behavior for a skipped Retro — while the consent amendment (engine-module.md) limits autonomous sends to bell/L1/L2/heartbeat, none of which can reference Retro state. The Crux likewise has no field in the MVP day record nor in the post-MVP conceptual day_records schema (technical-spec.md Addendum item 1), and no doc specifies how daily Crux contact is recorded. Two sub-claims are overstated: Crux adherence consequence IS designed (Green = "full Chain plus Crux," engine.md §2; engine-module.md Phase 9 anticipates Crux-inclusive Green scoring post-MVP), and queue nomination is a recorded Engine event, so "zero record it ever existed" is too strong; also the daily defenses (modes, floors, restart ritual, wired L1/L2) are Retro-independent, so only governance — not chain defense — dies with the Retro. Those caveats narrow but do not close the finding: the missed-Retro failure path, the Retro floor, and any Crux-contact record surface are absent from every doc.

checkAgainst: docs/calibration.md (Day-0 required outputs — whether any walkthrough, rehearsal, or comprehension step exists); docs/engine.md §2 and §8 (how modes/floors/budget are meant to reach the user); docs/mvp/engine-module.md (whether template copy is ever framed to the user as pre-committed/fixed).
citation: docs/engine.md §5 "Day 0 — definition of done"; docs/calibration.md (Day-0 required sections)
description: Day 0 successfully produces a valid config (bell, chain, floors, witness, stake, registry) but transfers no operational model of the relief valves the design depends on. Marcus ran every night as undeclared Green for three weeks — including two 14-hour heat-wave days — not from grit but from ignorance; modes, floor-scoring, and the budget were opaque until a fellow user explained them over the fence, and his first Red day and proper Yellow usage, the features that saved his adherence, arrived entirely via social transmission. Ruth's day-18 quit was reversed only when Marcus showed her his identical L1 text ('it's a form letter') — fixed templates read as personal judgments until a peer proves they are form letters — and her day-64 ankle survived at full streak only because she had watched Marcus's undeclared injury cost him $200 and he had said 'declare Red next time; the streak math doesn't know Saul died.' Fellow-userhood is the system's de facto comprehension and retention mechanism, and most users won't have it. Fixes: a comprehension check or rehearsed Yellow declaration during the baseline week; surface the form-letter nature of Engine copy to solo users; teach the relief valves as prominently as the commitments.
title: Onboarding produces configuration, not comprehension — the survival features arrive only by social transmission
verifierNote: The core claim is real: engine.md §5's Day-0 definition of done and calibration.md's required-for-Day-0 sections are purely configurational (bell, staging, witness, stake, ledger, registry, calendar) — no walkthrough, comprehension check, or rehearsed declaration exists anywhere, the baseline week (engine.md §2) is explicitly "run the chain, capture everything, change nothing," calibration.md never mentions Green/Yellow/Red at all (so the `lucid init` wizard that "asks these same questions" would never introduce modes), and undeclared days defaulting to Green (engine.md §2) has no documented counter-mechanism beyond the `/mode`-after-bell rejection string in engine-module.md's error table, which only fires if the user already knows `/mode` exists. However, the finding overstates floor opacity: the user authors an explicit Floor per Link at Day 0 (calibration.md "Active configuration"), the nightly close-out offers `floor` as a first-class answer, the partial-save message says "floor still counts," and the L1 template itself names the floor explicitly (engine.md §4) — floors are the one relief valve taught in-band. The form-letter claim is partially mitigated by design intent: P5 (architecture.md) frames escalation as "consent granted in advance, in writing, while strong," and the user personally sets `l1_enabled: true` against a consent table listing the fixed content (engine-module.md §"Consent amendment") — but no doc requires the wizard to show the user the actual L1/L2 text, and no template self-identifies as pre-committed at delivery. What remains open: a comprehension or rehearsal step at Day 0/baseline week, any onboarding path for modes and the error budget (currently reachable only by reading engine.md §2/§8), and surfacing the fixed-template nature of Engine copy to the user rather than only to the witness. One softening nuance on severity: MVP scoring is links-only (engine-module.md Phase 9: an undeclared floor-day scores 1.0), so undeclared-Green harm concentrates on full-miss/Red-day scenarios rather than every hard day.

checkAgainst: docs/observations.md §0 governing corollary and §9 Boundaries (no streaks/scores/targets clause — is a self-directed carve-out considered?); docs/architecture.md P3 (sanctuary scope); docs/mvp/product-principles.md (whether sustained low-mood series are addressed as concern or explicit non-concern anywhere).
citation: docs/observations.md §0 + §9; docs/architecture.md §3 (Engine reads)
description: The sanctuary rule — observations are inventory, never obligation; no reaction, no evaluation — is what made an addict willing to log cravings at all and made Sam safe to be honest post-breakup; that half is proven and must not be weakened. But the intersection of individually-correct refusals produces undesigned silence: Sam logged /mood 1-2 nightly for seven days during acute grief ('she left. logged.') to seven identical sub-second acks from every surface; Marcus's craving frequency quadrupled in week 4 (8 urge one-liners in 4 days after Darnell's cancellation) and the system was structurally forbidden from doing anything — including the one mechanism he explicitly requested: 'if I log ten urges in a week, tell ME — not Big Ed, me. The machine should be able to tap MY shoulder.' The docs prohibit any threshold/target/escalation on observations absolutely, never distinguishing 'the system grades you' from 'you pre-commit to informing yourself,' and no doc explicitly decides that no distress floor should exist — the silence is undesigned rather than designed. Marcus had AA and a sponsor to absorb the gap; a user without that scaffolding has nothing, and principled silence becomes indistinguishable from neglect. Note: any change touches architecture P3 and the sanctuary hard rule — the finding demands an explicit documented decision (either a carve-out for user-authored self-directed pings or a reasoned refusal), not an automatic weakening.
title: Sustained distress logging receives pure silence, and user-authored self-directed thresholds are forbidden rather than explicitly decided
verifierNote: The simulated behavior is real, not hallucinated: mood micro-logs are agent-free deterministic acks (observations.md §4; mvp/observations-module.md "Not agent-visible"), curiosity is "never a standalone ping" (§6), the autonomous-message ceiling is the three Engine templates plus heartbeat "not a precedent" (mvp/product-principles.md §7), and the cross-tree rule bars observation projections from every agent slice — so seven nights of /mood 1 structurally reach nothing. The self-directed threshold is genuinely forbidden without being deliberated: §0 bans "a streak, a quota, a target, a score, or an escalation" on any observation kind and §9 makes this "structural, not configurable" (so even P8 instance-overrides cannot reach it), never distinguishing system-grading from a user-pre-committed self-notification; §0's escape hatch ("becomes an Engine commitment through a Gate") cannot implement Marcus's request because the Engine "never reads Mirror content" (architecture.md §3), making an observation-count trigger unbuildable in any subsystem. The nearest explicit decisions are all adjacent, not on point: vision.md §6 decides no pushes for *absence* of logging ("no 'you were quiet' pushes"), architecture.md §6 decides the clinical boundary (pattern cartography, support professional care), observations.md §9 decides no diagnosis/advice — none addresses sustained distress that is actively logged, and no doc records a reasoned refusal of a distress floor or a self-directed carve-out. The finding's framing is also correct that the fix must be an explicit documented decision rather than an automatic weakening, since sanctuary is a hard rule in CLAUDE.md and P3.

checkAgainst: docs/vision.md §6-7 (relational promises, perspective entries and rings deferred); docs/calibration.md (Day-0 checklist — household disclosure absence); docs/mvp/agent-contracts.md People section (person-record minimalism and refusals); docs/mvp/product-principles.md (any cohabitant/disclosure guidance).
citation: docs/engine.md §"Day 0 — definition of done" + docs/calibration.md §"Witness arrangement"/§"Off-limits registry"
description: The most-documented person in Elena's Ledger has no existence in the system except a person_key silently accumulating ~140 entry_refs: no way to know what is held about him, no perspective entry, no reply channel, no consent story — while one spouse builds a longitudinal, structured, AI-summarized record of the marriage and the other holds memory alone. The MVP defers all of this to Phase 3-4, but the asymmetry costs are incurred immediately: Tomas's Day-17 discovery-by-ambush (his name on screen mid-/checkin, 'you talk to a robot about our marriage — and Maya gets reports') cost a fight, a protest-breach, an L2 that confirmed his accusation (finding 8), and nearly the system; Elena's Day-66 attempt to retrieve anything of his perspective hit /ask 'insufficient'; her mitigations (Sunday walks, the three-day rule, showing him the literal payload) were entirely self-invented. Day 0 elaborately covers witness, stake, environment, and off-limits registry, yet says nothing about cohabitants who will physically live alongside a bell labeled with the chain's name — there is no 'what to tell a partner' template despite the witness getting a formal briefing script, and no guidance on whether journaling-about-a-person norms belong in the Charter or the off-limits registry. Even guidance-level doc material would close the acute gap without pulling Phase-3 features forward.
title: The asymmetric-adoption household has no story: non-users live with the bell, dominate the record, and get nothing — costs land in month one
verifierNote: The Day-0 definition of done (engine.md §7) exhaustively lists Bell label, staged environment, witness briefing, stake, ledger location, off-limits registry, Away Mode, and scheduled reviews — and calibration.md gives the witness a formal one-paragraph briefing script — yet neither doc, nor product-principles.md, nor any doc in the repo, contains a single word about disclosing to a cohabitant, a "what to tell a partner" template, or where journaling-about-a-person norms belong (a repo-wide grep for household/cohabitant/spouse/roommate/disclosure guidance returns nothing at guidance level). The person record itself is exactly as the finding describes: data-model.md §"People references" specifies a mutable JSON silently merging entry_refs with no subject-side visibility, reply, or consent mechanism, and agent-contracts.md §"People (extractive)" forbids everything except accumulation; every counterweight — perspective entries, rings/apertures, shared profiles — is explicitly deferred (vision.md §7 Ingest, §9 Future Possibilities; mvp/README.md §"What this MVP is not": "Not a shared-profile network"). The simulated behaviors are doc-consistent, not hallucinated: steel-thread.md shows person names echoed on screen during /checkin and /reflect ("When M. is in the room..."), and /ask returning "insufficient" for anything outside validated insights is specified (agent-contracts.md §3, error-states.md R-10). The only partial mitigations the docs offer are structural, not disclosive — low-signal person_key slugs (data-model.md §"person_key derivation") and the topic-level off-limits registry (architecture.md §5), which never says whether a person can be an excluded topic — so the acute month-one asymmetry cost lands exactly as the finding claims, with no guidance-level material to close it.
